question_structure.context: Place two identical wooden blocks A and B, each with a mass of 4 kg, on a horizontal table. Under the action of a horizontal pulling force $F_1$ of 16 N, blocks A and B move together in uniform linear motion. (Take $g = 10\mathrm{N/kg}$)
question_structure.sub_question_1: At this moment, what is the magnitude of the frictional force $F_{f1}$ on the upper surface of block B?
question_structure.sub_question_2: What is the frictional force $F_{f2}$ on the lower surface of B?
question_structure.sub_question_3: What is the coefficient of kinetic friction between the tabletop and the wooden block?

sub_question_1.step_1: A is moving at a constant velocity. According to the equilibrium condition, the friction force on A is zero, so the upper surface of B has $F_{f1} = 0$.

sub_question_2.step_2: Analyzing the forces on B, according to the equilibrium condition, in the horizontal direction, we have: $F_{f2} = F_{1} = 16 \mathrm{N}$

sub_question_3.step_3: Taking the AB system as the object of study, in the vertical direction we have: $F_{N} = (m_{A} + m_{B})g$

image_captions: Two rectangular blocks, labeled A and B, are stacked vertically on a horizontal surface. A horizontal force, labeled F1, is applied to the right side of block B.

step_1.physical_theorem: Equilibrium condition for uniform linear motion

result_quantity.name: Frictional force on the upper surface of block B

step_2.physical_theorem: Equilibrium condition for uniform linear motion

result_quantity.name: Frictional force on the lower surface of B

step_3.physical_theorem: Equilibrium condition in the vertical direction

result_quantity.name: Normal force on the AB system
Frictional force on the lower surface of B
Coefficient of kinetic friction between the tabletop and the wooden block
